2604.07965.title: DSCA: Dynamic Subspace Concept Alignment for Lifelong VLM Editing
2604.07965.paper_summary: Model editing aims to update knowledge to add new concepts and change relevant information without retraining. Lifelong editing is a challenging task, prone to disrupting previously learned concepts, especially for Vision Language Models (VLMs), because sequential edits can lead to degraded reasoning and cross modal misalignment. Existing VLM knowledge editing methods based on gated adapters, activation edits, and parameter merging techniques address catastrophic forgetting seen in full fine tuning; however, they still operate in the shared representation space of the VLM, where concepts are entangled, so edits interfere with other non relevant concepts. We hypothesize that this instability persists because current methods algorithmically control edits via optimization rather than structurally separating knowledge. We introduce Dynamic Subspace Concept Alignment (DSCA) which by design mitigates this limitation by decomposing the representation space into a set of orthogonal semantic subspaces and proposing edits only in those transformed spaces. These subspaces are obtained through incremental clustering and PCA on joint vision language representations. This process structurally isolates concepts, enabling precise, non interfering edits by turning isolation from a soft training objective into an architectural property. The surgical edits are guided by a multi term loss function for maintaining task fidelity, edit locality, and cross modal alignment. With the base model frozen, our method achieves 98 percent single edit success, remains over 95 percent after 1000 sequential edits, lowers hallucination by 3 to 5 percent, and achieves the best backward transfer (BWT) scores on continual instruction tuning benchmarks. Extensive experiments demonstrate DSCA state of the art stability and knowledge retention capability in continual lifelong editing across various datasets and benchmarks.
2604.07965.paper_summary_zh: 模型編輯旨在更新知識，以添加新概念並改變相關信息，而無需重新訓練。終身編輯是一項具有挑戰性的任務，容易干擾先前學習的概念，尤其是對於視覺語言模型（VLMs），因為連續編輯可能導致推理能力下降和跨模態不對齊。現有的基於閘控適配器、激活編輯和參數合併技術的VLM知識編輯方法解決了在完全微調中出現的災難性遺忘；然而，它們仍然在VLM的共享表示空間中運作，這裡的概念是交織在一起的，因此編輯會干擾其他不相關的概念。我們假設這種不穩定性持續存在，因為當前的方法通過優化算法控制編輯，而不是結構性地分離知識。我們引入了動態子空間概念對齊（DSCA），它通過將表示空間分解為一組正交語義子空間來設計，以減輕這一限制，並僅在這些轉換的空間中提出編輯。這些子空間是通過對聯合視覺語言表示進行增量聚類和主成分分析（PCA）獲得的。這一過程結構性地隔離了概念，使得通過將隔離從一個柔性訓練目標轉變為一個架構性屬性來實現精確且不干擾的編輯。外科編輯由多項損失函數指導，以維持任務的保真度、編輯的局部性和跨模態對齊。在基礎模型凍結的情況下，我們的方法實現了98%的單次編輯成功率，在1000次連續編輯後仍保持在95%以上，降低了3%到5%的幻覺，並在持續指令微調基準上達到了最佳的向後轉移（BWT）分數。大量實驗證明，DSCA在各種數據集和基準上的持續終身編輯中展現了最先進的穩定性和知識保留能力。
2604.07965.author: Gyanendra Das et.al.
